dialog: ...the fuck...
Yeah?
Hi, Miles. Can you move your car, please?
What for?
The painters got to put the truck in, and you didn’t park too good.
Yeah, hold on.
Fuck!

Yeah, no, I know I said I’d be there by noon, but there’s been all this work going on at my building, and it’s like a total nightmare, and I had a bunch of stuff to deal with this morning. I’m out the door right this second. It’s going to be great. Yeah. Bye.
Hey, Miles.
Hey, Simon. Triple espresso, please.
Rough night, huh? For here?
No, I’m running late. Make it to go. And give me a New York Times and...a spinach croissant.
Look what the cat dragged!
Hi, everybody.
About time you got here, bud. Mr. Prompt.
We were thinking maybe you took the wrong way and went to Tijuana and they didn’t let you back in.
I had to bribe them.
Hey, Miles.
Seriously though, the 405 was unbelievable today. Unbelievable. Bumper to bumper the whole way. People getting an early start on the weekend, I guess. Granted I got a late start, but still.
Christine, why don’t you ask Miles about the cake?
Oh. Good idea. Here, Miles, come to the kitchen with me.
Oh hey. Don’t bother him with that. We got to get going.
It’ll just take a second.
Jack tells us you are publishing a book. Congratulations.
Yeah, well, it’s not exactly finalized yet, but, um, there has been some interest and --
Your friend is modest.
Yeah, Miles, don’t be so modest. Indulge them. Don’t make me out a liar.
What subject is your book? Non- fiction?
No, it’s a novel. Fiction. Although there’s a lot from my own life, so I guess technically a lot of it is non-fiction.
I like non-fiction. There is so much to know about the world that I think reading a story someone just invented is kind of a waste of time.
So which one do you like better?
I like them both.
See?
Where the fuck were you, man? I was dying in there. We were supposed to be a hundred miles away by now.
I can’t help the traffic.
Come on. You’re fucking hungover.
Okay, there was a tasting last night. But I wanted to get us some stuff for the ride up. Check out the box.
Why did you tell them my book was being published?
You said you had it all lined up.
No, I didn’t. What I said was that my agent heard there was some interest at Conundrum...
Yeah, Conundrum.

...and that one of the editors was passing it up to a senior editor. She was supposed to hear something this week, but now it’s a week, and... It’s always like this. It’s always a fucking waiting game.
I don’t know. Senior Editor? Sounds like you’re in to me.

It’s a long shot, all right? I’ve been rejected by seventeen other publishers, and I’m not getting my hopes up again. And Conundrum is just a small specialty publisher anyway. I’ve stopped caring. That’s it. I’ve stopped caring. So just do me a favor -- don’t bring it up again.

Well, I had to tell her parents something. I mean hey, I’m proud of you. But Christine’s parents, they sort of, you know, they kind of play in the big leagues, and they like people with a little ambition.  And you have it, man. You have it. I just --
Don’t open that now. It’s warm.
Come on, we’re celebrating. I say we pop it.
That’s a 1992 Vintage Byron. It’s sacrilegious.
See what I mean? It’s pissed off.
Shut up. Here’s to a great week.
Absolutely. An outstanding week. I’m glad we’re getting this time together.
Oh, that’s tasty.
100% Pinot Noir. Single vineyard.
Pinot Noir? How come it’s white?
Jesus! Don’t ask questions like that up in wine country. They’ll think you’re a moron.
Just tell me.
Color in red wines comes from the skins. This juice is free run, so there’s no skin contact in the fermentation, ergo no color.
Sure is tasty.
Did you read the latest draft, by the way?
Oh yeah. Yeah.
And?
I liked it a lot. A lot of improvements. It just seemed overall, I don’t know, tighter, more... congealed or something.
How about the new ending? Did you like that?
Oh yeah. Much better.
There is no new ending. Page 750 on is exactly the same.
Well, then I guess it must have felt new because everything leading up to it was so different.
What the fuck? It’s Christine. Hey you.
You guys having fun?
Yeah. All five minutes so far have been a blast.
Good. That’s good.
So what’s up?

Just seeing how you’re doing. And, um, Mom and I were starting to look over the seating charts again, and we’re wondering if you wanted Tony Levin to sit next to the Feldmans, or should he be at one of the singles tables? So what do you think? With the Feldmans?
Yeah, the Feldmans.
The problem with that is that then there’s one extra --
Well, then put him at the singles table.
Then put him with the Feldmans. I don’t care. Whatever you and your Mom decide is fine with me.
Don’t dismiss me. I’m trying to include you in this decision. He’s your friend.
I didn’t dismiss you. I told you what I thought, but it didn’t seem to matter, so you decide. And this is supposed to be my time with Miles anyway, so please try not to call every five minutes.
I’m not going to call every five minutes, but this is important

Look, all I know is I just got on the road with my best man, and you call before we’re even on the freeway. I just hope you’re going to give me a little space before the wedding. Isn’t that the point of this? Isn’t that what we talked about with Dr. Gertler?
Why are you being so defensive?
Because I feel attacked.
So now I’m attacking you. I ask you one simple question, and suddenly I’m attacking you.
Listen. I’ll call you when we get there, and we’ll talk about it then, okay? Okay?
I love you.
Fucking Bride-zilla!
You invited Tony Levin?
Why are we getting off?
Um...I’ve just got to make one quick stop. Won’t take a second.
What?
My mother. She’s here in Montecito. Right off the freeway.
Your mother? Jesus, Miles, we’re supposed to be up there already.
It’s her birthday tomorrow. And I don’t feel right driving by her house and not stopping in to say happy birthday, okay? It’ll just take a second.
How old?
Um...seventy...something.
Did you get her a present?
I’ll just give her a bottle.
A bottle? That’s no present for your mother. She gave birth to you, for Christ’s sake.
I’m supposed to thank her for that?
I’ll be right back.
Fuck, I forgot.
Here, sign this.
Happy birthday, Mom.
Yeah, happy birthday.
My God. Miles. And Jack! What a surprise. I can’t remember the last time you brought me flowers, Miles.
They’re from both of us.
A famous actor bringing me flowers on my birthday. Don’t I feel special?
A famous actor who’s getting married next week.
Oh, that’s right. Isn’t that nice. I hope that girl knows how lucky she is, marrying no less than Derek Summersby.
Jeez, Mrs. Raymon, that was eleven years ago.
Well, you were wonderful on that show. I never understood why they had to give you that brain tumor so soon. Why that didn’t make you the biggest movie star in the world is a sin. It’s a sin
Yeah, well, you should be my agent.

If I was, I would sing your praises up and down the street until they put me in the loony bin. Now Miles, why didn’t you tell me you were coming and bringing along this handsome man? Look how I’m dressed. I’ve got to run and put my face on.
You look fabulous, Mrs. Raymond.
Oh, stop it. Make yourselves comfortable. Have you eaten yet?
No.
We’re not going to stay too long, right?
Yeah. We’ll just hang out a little while and hit the road.
Okay. Cool.
Let me show you something. The secret to opening champagne is that once the cork is released, you keep pressure on it so you don’t --
Just a second.
Ready for my close up!
Oh, champagne! Miles, why don’t you bring that out onto the lanai? I thought we could eat on the lanai.
Mrs. Raymond, this is delicious. Absolutely delicious.

They’re just leftovers. I could have made something fancier if a certain someone had let me know that a certain someone was coming for a visit with a certain special friend. Could have made my pork roast.
It’s a surprise, Mom.

And I could have already put clean sheets on the other bed and the fold-out. You are staying. Wendy, Ron, and the twins are picking us up at 11:30 to go to Sunday brunch at the Sheraton. They do a magnificent job there. Wendy is so excited you’re here.
You talked to Wendy?
Just now. She’s so thrilled. And the kids.
Yeah, well. You know, Jack’s pretty eager to get up to... you know, but, uh, yeah. We’ll see how it goes.
Well, you boys do what you want. I just think it would be nice for us to be together as a family.
Uh-huh. I’ll be right back.
Seven, eight, nine, ten, eleven, twelve, thirteen, fourteen, fifteen... Sixteen.
And what was that other one you did, the one where you’re the jogger?
Oh, that was for, uh, wait...That was for Spray and Wash.
Spray and Wash. That’s the one.
Yeah, I remember the girl who was in it with me. Yikes, she was something.
I don’t remember her. I just remember you jogging.
How come a handsome man like you is not married yet?
Jack’s getting married next week, Mom, remember?
And Miles is my best man. My main man.
Yeah, yeah. And your wife works in real estate. With her father. Of course I remember. Miles, when are you going to get married again?
Mom, I just got divorced.
You and Victoria should get back together. She was good for you. Because I saw something on TV about how people who live alone and don’t believe in God live shorter lives.
Much shorter. She was good for you. And so beautiful and intelligent. You knew her, right?
Oh, yeah. Very well. Still do.
I’m worried about you, Miles.
Jack. Jack.
She’ll wake up.
Fuck, man. Too early in the morning for that, you know what I mean?
She’s a kid. I don’t even look at that stuff anymore.
That’s your problem right there.
We have nothing in common with her. You get her in bed a couple of times and then what?
I don’t know.
As if she’d even be attracted to guys like us in the first place.
I get chicks looking at me all the time. All ages.
It’s not worth it. You pay too big a price. It’s never free.
You need to get laid.
It’d be the best thing for you. You know what? I’m going to get you laid this week. I’m no going to give you a pen knife or a gift certificate. That’s going to be my best man gift to you.
I’d rather have a knife.

I am not going to watch my best friend just waste away. You’ve been officially depressed for like two years now, and you were always a negative guy anyway, even back in college. Now it’s even worse. You’re wasting away. Teaching English to fucking eighth-graders when they should be reading what you wrote. Your books.
I’m working on it.
You still seeing that shrink?
I went on Monday, but I spent most of the hour helping him with his computer.
Well, I say fuck therapy and what’s that stuff you take, Xanax?
And Lexapro, yes.
Well, I say fuck that. You need to get your joint worked on, that’s what you need.

Jack. This week is not about me. It’s about you. I’m going to show you a good time. We’re going to drink a lot of good wine, enjoy the beauty of the central coast, eat great food, and send you off in style.
And get you back in the saddle.
You know what? Let’s take the turnoff to Santa Rosa and hit Sanford first.
Whatever’s closest. I need a glass.
These guys make a top-notch Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. One of the best producers in Santa Barbara county. Look how beautiful this view is. What a day!
I thought you hated Chardonnay.
No, I like all varietals. I just don’t generally like the way they manipulate Chardonnay in California -- too much oak and secondary malolactic fermentation.

Jesus, what a day! Isn’t it gorgeous? And the ocean’s right over that ridge. See, the reason this region’s good for Pinot is that the cold air off the Pacific flows in at night through these transverse valleys and cools down the berries. Pinot doesn’t like constant heat, and it really despises humidity because it’s thin skinned and susceptible to disease and rot.
Hey, Miles, I hope your novel sells.
I do too. Thanks. Here we are.
So what’d you guys finally decide on for the menu?
I told you. Filet and salmon.
Yeah, but how are they making the salmon? Poached with a yogurt-dill sauce? Teriyaki? Curry?
I don’t know. Salmon. Don’t you always have white wine with fish?
Oh Jesus. Look, at some point we have to find out because it’s going to make a big difference.
Let me call Christine.
Doesn’t have to be now. Let’s go taste.
I owe her a call.

Hey, honey. So we’re up here about to taste some whites, and we need to know how the caterers are going to make the salmon...I know, I didn’t forget, but we wound up at Miles’s mom’s house, and it got really late, and it was hard to call, so I’m calling you now. I said I was sorry. Yes, I did. You heard me say I was sorry, right?

Give me a break, will you? I just called to find out about the salmon -- for our wedding -- to be more involved, like you said -- and all you want to do is get into it with me about last night and, okay, I’m sorry. I’m sorry I didn’t call. You’re totally right. I know, but I’m trying to make this the best wedding I can with the best wine we can find up here.
Baked with a butter-lime glaze.
Now we’re talking.
This is your buddy?
Yep, this is the bridegroom. Jack, Chris. Chris, Jack.
How you doing?
You guys want to start with the Sauvignon Blanc?
Sounds good.
Let me show you.
First take your glass and examine the wine against the light. Look at the color and clarity.
What color is it supposed to be?
Depends on the wine. Just get a sense of it. Thick? Thin? Straw? Amber? Yellow? Watery? Syrupy?
Huh.

Now tip it. What you’re doing here is checking for density of color as it thins toward the rim. Tells you how old it is, among other things, usually more important with reds. This is a very young wine, so it’s going to retain its color pretty solidly. Now stick your nose in it.
Don’t be shy. Get your nose in there. Pretend it’s young pussy.
What do you smell?
Uh...wine? White wine? I don’t know.
There’s not much there yet, but you can still find...
Huh. I’m getting a little peach. Yeah, peach. I’m not so sure about the cheese.
Now set your glass down and get some air into it.
Oxygenating it opens it up, unlocks the aroma and the flavors. Very important. Now we smell again.
See? That’s what I want you to do with every one.
When do we get to drink it?
Now.
So how would you rate this one?
Usually they start you on the wines with learning disabilities, but this one’s pretty damn good.
You know, you could work in a wine store.
Yeah, that would be a real step up from teaching. Plus, I’ve got six points on my license already.
Are you chewing gum?
Want some?
Hey Jack, hurry up!
Just a minute!
I thought you said it was close. Now I’m all pitted out.
It’s not even a mile.
We should have driven.
Not with the wine list these people have. We don’t want to be shy.
You think I’m making a mistake marrying Christine?
Whoa, where’s that coming from?
I don’t know. Do you think I’m doing the right thing? Tell the truth.
I think it’s great. It’s time. And Christine is great. You’ve got to have your eyes open, that’s all. I mean, look at me. I thought Victoria and I were set for life.

Christine’s dad -- he’s been talking about making room for me in his business. Showing me the ropes. And I’m thinking about it. But I don’t know, might get a little incestuous. On the other hand, Mike does pretty well. A lot of high-end commercial stuff. Writes his own paycheck.
You’d stop acting?
Not entirely. This would provide some stability is all I’m saying. I could always squeeze in an audition or a commercial here and there, keep myself in the game in case something big comes along.
Wow. That’s a big change.
We’re not getting any younger, right? And I guess my career, it’s gotten pretty, you know, cyclical. Time to settle down.
If that’s what feels right.
It does. Feels right.
Then it’s a good thing.
Yeah. It’s good. Feels good.
Hey, Miles. Long time no see.
Gary.
When’s that novel of yours coming out? We all want to read it.
Soon, soon. Say, this is my buddy Jack. He’s getting married next week.
My condolences.
What are you pouring tonight?
Lot of good stuff.. Got a 2000 Bien Nacido. Want a taste?
Absolutely. They have their own label that’s just outstanding.
What do you think?
Tighter than a nun’s asshole but good concentration. Jack?
Yeah. Tight.
Pour us a couple.
Here’s to my last week of freedom.
Yep.
Miles. Check it out.
Oh yeah. That’s Maya.
You know her?
Sure I know Maya.
You know that chick?
Jack, this is where I eat when I come up here. It’s practically my office. And sometimes I have a drink with the employees. Maya’s great. She’s worked here about a year, maybe a year and a half.
She is hot.
And married. Check out the rock.
Doesn’t mean shit. When Christine was a hostess at Sushi Roku, she wore a big engagement ring to keep guys from hitting on her. Think it worked? Fuck no. How do you think I met her?
This gal’s married to some professor at UC Santa Barbara.
So what’s a professor’s wife doing waitressing? Obviously that’s over.
You don’t know anything about this woman. Calm down. Let’s just eat. The duck is excellent and pairs nicely with the Highliner.
What else do you know about her?
Well...she knows a lot about wine.
Oooooohhh. Now we’re getting somewhere.
And she like Pinot.
There you go.
Jack, she’s a fuckign waitress in Buellton.
Good enough for me.
Hey, Miles. Good to see you.
How are you, Maya? Busy night.
Saturday. You guys been out tasting today?
You know it. This is my friend Jack. Jack, Maya.
Hiya.
Hi. I’ll see you guys later.
Jesus, she’s jammin’. And you know her.
Yeah, I know a waitress here. So what?
Gee, Miles, I don’t know. Maybe she has a friend. You know, two girls, two guys, small town, Pinot... Did you see how friendly she was to us?
She’s friendly to everyone. Good for the gratuities.
You’re blind, dude. Blind.
I also recommend the ostrich. Very lean. Locally raised.
Did you know that I hate Tony Levin?
Highliner, please.
That’s on us.
Hey, guys.
You want to join us?
Sure.
So how’s that book of yours going, Miles? I think you were almost done with it last time we talked.
I finished it.
Good for you.
It’s getting published. That’s why we’re up here celebrating.
That’s fantastic! Congratulations.
Are you a writer too?
No, I’m an actor.
Oh yeah? What kind of stuff?
Mostly TV. I was a regular on a couple of series. But I’d have to say commercials are my bread and butter. National mostly.
Anything I’d know?
Maybe. Recognize this?
“Now with low, low 5.8 APR financing.”
That’s hilarious. You sound just like one of those guys.
I am one of those guys!
You are not.
He is.

Consult your doctor before using this product. Side effects may include dizziness, hives, loss of appetite, difficulty breathing and low blood pressure. If you have diabetes or a history of kidney trouble... you’re fucked!
Oh. I needed that. Thank you.
So what are you guys up to tonight?
We’re pretty wiped. Probably go back to the hotel and crash.
Yeah, I know what you mean. It’s a long drive up here. Where’re you staying?
The Windmill.
Windmill.
Well, good to see you, Miles. Jack.
See you.
We’ll catch up with you later, okay?
We’ll probably go back to the hotel and crash?
The girl is looking to party, and you tell her we’re going to go back to our motel room on a Saturday night and crash? Jesus, Miles!
Well, I’m tired. Aren’t you tired?
The chick digs you. She lit up like a pinball machine when she heard your novel was getting published.
Now I’ve got another lie to live down. Thanks, Jack.
I’m trying to get you some action, but you’ve got to help me out.
Didn’t seem to me like that’s what was going on. You were all over her.
Somebody had to do the talking. And by the way, I was right. She’s not married.
How do you know?
No rock. When she came to the bar, no rock.
Single. Waitress. Saturday night. Looking for love. A little slap and tickle.
Shut up.
She probably went home, lit some candles, put on some relaxing music, took a nice hot bath, and laid down on her bed with her favorite vibrator.
Have you no shame?
Oooh. Oh. Miles. Miles.
Fuck you.
Shut the fuck up!
You should get your prostate checked.
What’re we going to have? The 12- egg omelete? Pigs in a blanket? The “rancher’s special breakfast?” Or maybe just some grease and fat with a side of lard?
Yeah. So what’s the plan today?
We head north, begin our grape tour up there, make our way south so the more we drink the closer we get to the motel.
What’s your problem?
What is it?
I am going to get my nut on this trip, Miles. And you are not going to fuck it up for me with all your depression and anxiety and neg-head downer shit.
Ooooh, now the cards are on the table.
Yes they are. And I’m serious. Do not fuck with me. I am going to get laid before I settle down on Saturday. Do you read me?
Sure, big guy. Whatever you say. It’s your party. I’m sorry I’m in the way and dragging you down. Maybe you’d have a better time on your own. You take the car. I can catch a bus back.
No, see, I want both of us to get crazy. We should both be cutting loose. I mean, this is our last chance. This is our week! It should be something we share.
Can I take your order?
But I am warning you.
Oatmeal, one poached eggs, and rye toast. Dry.
Okay. And you?
Pigs in a blanket. With extra syrup.
How much skin and stem contact?
About four weeks.
Huh. So that’s where you get all the tannin. And how long in oak?
A year -- half in French, half in American.
Wow. Good stuff.
Very good oak. That’s a good wood.
Nice, huh?
Beautiful
Victoria and I used to like this view. Once we had a picnic here and drank a ‘95 Opus One. With smoked salmon and artichokes, but we didn’t care.
Miles.
She has the best palate of any woman I’ve ever known. She could even differentiate Italian wines.
Miles, I gotta tell you something. Victoria’s coming to the wedding.
I know. You told me. I’m okay with it.
Yeah, but that’s not the whole story. She got remarried.
She what? When?
About a month ago. Six weeks.
To that guy? That guy with the restaurant...
Yeah.
...with the lousy wine list?
I had to tell you sooner or later.
Miles...MILES... Jesus Christ, Miles! Get out!
I want to go home now.
You’ve been divorced for two years already. People move on. She has! It’s like you enjoy self-pity. Makes you feel special or something.
Is she bringing him to the wedding?
What do you think?
You drop this bombshell on me. Why didn’t you tell me before?

Because I knew you’d freak out and probably get so depressed you wouldn’t even come on this trip. But then I figured here would be the best place to tell you. We’re here to forget about all that shit. We’re here to party!
I’m going to be a fucking pariah on Saturday. Persona non-grata. Everyone’s just going to be holding their breath to see if I’m going to get drunk and make a scene.
No, no, no. It’s cool. I talked to Victoria. She’s cool. Everyone’s cool.
You’ve all been talking about it? Behind my back? Talking about it?
Hey, hey, hey. No, you don’t!
Miles?
You going to be okay?
Come on. Let’s go.
Hey, guys. How’s it going?
Excellent. My friend and I are up here doing the wine tour, and he tells me that you folks make one hell of a Pinot.
That’s what people say.
You gotta excuse him. Yesterday he didn’t know Pinot Noir from film noir.
But I’m learning fast.
I’m trying to teach my friend here some basics about wine over the next few days before he goes off and --

That’s right -- I’m here to learn. I never had that much interest in wine before, but this trip as been very enlightening. Always liked wine, of course, but I don’t know. Always more of a beer man. Microbreweries and such.
Well, no better way to learn than tasting.
Now here’s a girl who knows how to pour. What’s your name?
Stephanie.
So what do you think?
Quaffable but far from transcendent.
I like it. Tastes great. Oaky.
Cabernet Franc. This is only the fifth year we’ve made this varietal. It’s from our Tinaquaic vineyard. And it was a Silver Medal winner at the Paso Robles wine festival last year.
Well, I’ve come to never expect greatness from a Cab Franc, and this one’s no exception. Sort of flabby, overripe, thin --
Tastes pretty damn good to me. You live around here, Stephanie?
Just outside Santa Ynez. And I agree with you about the Cab Franc.
Oh yeah? That’s where we’re staying. Windmill Inn.
Oh yeah.
You know a gal named Maya? Works at the Hitching Post?
Sure I know Maya. Real well.
No shit. We just had a drink with her last night. Miles knows her.
Could we move on to the Pinot, please?
Chomping at the bit, huh? Sure.
You’re a bad, bad girl, Stephanie.
I know. I need to be spanked.
Excuse me.
Yeeeaah. I’m going to get this whole thing lined up.
What whole thing?
You. Me. Stephanie. Maya.
Do you know how often these pourers get hit on? Especially the cute ones?
Please.
Get the trunk.
You have the keys.
We’re on.
What?
She called Maya, who’s not working tonight, so we’re all going out.
Tonight?
Sooner the better.
Chick has got it all going on.
She’s a cutie all right.
Cutie? She’s a fucking hottie. And you almost tell her I’m getting married. What’s the matter with you? (drumming the steering wheel) Gotta love it. Gotta love it.
You know how often these pourers get hit on?
Nah. I want to watch this.
Miles. Hey, Miles. Time to get up.
Fucking chick in the Jacuzzi -- goddamn, Miles, fucking going nuts up here. The whole place is wide open.
So what should I wear?
I don’t know. Casual but nice. They think you’re a writer.
Don’t you have any other shoes?
Hello? Oh hey, baby, just checking in. Ah, not much. We’re about to go out for dinner, probably be out pretty late, so I thought I’d say goodnight. I know, I miss you too...
Please just try to be your normal humorous self, okay? Like you were before the tailspin. Remember? People love that guy. And don’t forget -- your novel is coming out in the fall.
What’s it called?
Don’t sabotage me. If you want to be a lightweight, that’s your call. But do not sabotage me.
Aye-aye, captain.
And if they want to drink Merlot, we’re drinking Merlot.
If anyone orders Merlot, I’m leaving! I am not drinking any fucking Merlot!
Okay, okay. Relax, Miles, Jesus. No Merlot. Did you bring your Xanax?
And don’t drink too much. I don’t want you going to the dark side or passing out. Do you hear me? No going to the dark side.
Okay! Fuck!
We’re going in.
How you doin’ tonight, beautiful?
Good. How’re you?
Great. You look great. You both do.
Not so bad yourself.
What are you drinking?
A Sea Smoke Reserve.
Oh yeah? How is it?
Try it.
Nice. Very nice.
I know the vintner. Comes in the restaurant all the time. Only makes 300 cases.
This is good. Little hints of clove.
I know. I love that.

...medallions of pork with a dusting of black truffles served with a root vegetable foulon and wasabi-whipped potatoes. And finally a Copper River salmon grilled on an alder wood plank. And that comes with roasted new potatoes and steamed watercress.
And who gets the wine list?
I guess Miles wants it.
Nope. You ladies choose.
You choose, Stephanie.
So what does everyone feel like?

That helps.
Don’t give up. I know you’re going to make it. I’m betting good money on it.

I’m forty-two. Half my life is over, and I have nothing to show for it. I’m a thumbprint on the first floor window of a skyscraper. I’m a smudge of excrement on a tissue surging out to sea with a million tons of raw sewage.
See? Right there. Just what you just said. That’s beautiful. A thumbprint on a skyscraper. I couldn’t write that.
Me neither. I think it’s Bukowski.
Oh, look! There’s Steph!
Hey, baby. Looking good.
Look what I got for our favorite girl.
Stephanie! Stop!
You fucking bastard! Lying piece of shit! You’re getting married on Saturday? What was all that shit you said to me?
I can explain.
I thought you loved me! You fuck! I hope you die!
Fuckface!
Aren’t you glad you didn’t move up here and marry her?
Don’t need a fucking lecture. Who told her, anyway? You fucking told Maya, didn’t you?
No, I did not. I’m thinking it must have been Gary at the Hitching Post. I’m pretty sure we told him the first night.
You told him. All I know is I’m fucking hurting here.
Keep it elevated.

It’s Miles. Listen, I don’t know if you even care, but I had to call and tell you again how immensely I enjoyed our time together and how sorry I am that I let you down. I think you’re great, Maya -- always have. From the first time you waited on me. I should also tell you that my book is not getting published. I thought this one had a chance, but I just got the bad news from my agent. That makes me three for three. So don’t bother reading it -- you’ve got better things to do. Seems like I’m not much of a writer. I’m not anything. In fact, the only real talent I seem to have is for disappointing people, and now you know that first hand. We’re leaving in the morning, and I want you to know that I will take with me wonderful memories of you -- last night and this morning chief among them.
Well?
I’m going to need an operation. Maybe a couple of them. They have to wait for it to heal first. Then they break it again.
Good thing you have a voice-over career.
Might fuck that up too. I should sue her ass. Only reason I won’t is to protect Christine.
That’s thoughtful.
Yeah.
I really hate this place.
Take a couple of these, and you’ll learn to love it.
Two for you. And two for me.
So how did Stephanie find out? It’s not like she works with Gary.
Simple. Gary must have told Maya, and Maya told Stephanie.
But how did Stephanie know it was Saturday? We didn’t get into that with Gary, did we?
Let me think.
You sure you didn’t say anything to Maya?

Sure I’m sure. And just what are you implying? I’m really pissed off at you about all this, if you want to know the truth. What’s Maya going to think of me now just for association with you? You’re the one who’s sabotaging me, not the other way around, pal. Not by a longshot.
I don’t know. Just seems fishy.
What’s it look like to you?
Looks like you were in an accident.
I’m hungry.
You know what I’m thinking?
What?
I’m thinking it’s time to settle down. One woman. One house. You know. It’s time.
Well, you are getting married day after tomorrow, so...
Mm. Mm.
And here’s your Handi-wipes.
Oh, so that’s what those things are? For a second there I thought you  guys were handing out condoms.
I’ll be right back with more corn bread.
I bet you that chick is two tons of fun. You know, the grateful type.
Um...I don’t know. I wouldn’t know.
If I were you, I’d be all over that like white on rice.
Nice technique there... ...Cammi.
It’s all in the wrist. You know, you look really familiar. You from around here? Where’d you go to high school?
We’re from San Diego. Why?
I don’t know. You just seem really familiar to me for some reason. Never mind. Enjoy your meals.
Hang on. Did you ever know a Derek Sommersby?
Dr. Derek Sommersby? You mean from “One Life to Live?”
You have to imagine him with a bandage and shorter hair.
And about twenty pounds heavier.
No. Way. No way!
Oh, my God!
Could you tell me where the bathroom is?
Uh, sure, it’s right over there, second left past the buffalo.
She gets off in an hour, so I think I’m just going to have a drink and then...make sure she gets home safe.
You’re joking, right? What are you doing? Unbelievable. Can’t we just go back to the hotel and hang out and we get up early and play nine holes before we check out?

Look, Miles. I know you’re my friend and you care about me. And I know you disapprove. I respect that. But there are some things I have to do that you don’t understand. You understand wine and literature and movies, but I don’t think you understand my plight.
Your plight?
Jesus fucking Christ, it’s freezing.
Vicodin. Where’s the Vicodin?
Fucking chick’s married.
What?
Her husband works a night shift or something, and he comes home, and I’m on the floor with my cock in his wife’s ass.
Jesus, Jack. Jesus. And you walked all the way back from Solvang?
Ran. Twisted my ankle too.
That’s five clicks, Jackson.
Fucking-a it’s five clicks! At one point I cut through an ostrich farm. Fuckers are mean.
And I left my wallet.
So?
My credit cards, cash, fucking ID, everything. Everything. We gotta go back.
Don’t worry. We’ll call right now and cancel your cards.
The wedding bands. The wedding bands are in my wallet.
Okay, so they were in your wallet, and you left your wallet somewhere. Christine’ll understand.

She ordered them special. Took her forever to find them. They’ve got this design engraved on them with dolphins and our names engraved in Sanskrit. We’ve got to go back. Christine’ll fucking crucify me.
You’re talking about walking into a hornet’s nest. We’re not going.
Please, Miles, please.
No way. Get it out of your mind. Look, your wallet was stolen at a bar. Happens every day.
No! We’ve got to get my wallet! Those rings are irreplaceable! We’ve got to get them, Miles! I fucked up! I know I fucked up, okay? I fucked up. You gotta help me. You gotta help me. Pleeeease!
Oh, God, please... Oh, God. I know I’m bad. I know I did a bad thing. Help me, Miles. Just this one thing, just this one last thing. Please, Miles, please....uuuuu.... Uuuuuu....uuuuuuu......
She tell you she was married?
Yeah.
So what the fuck were you thinking?
Wasn’t supposed to be back till six. Fucker rolls in at five.
Cutting it a little close, weren’t you? So how was she? Compared to Stephanie, say.
Horny as shit. Flopping around like a landed trout.
Yeah, this is the block. Just keep going... Yeah! This is it. There’s her car.
So what’s the plan?
You go.
Me?
Yeah, my ankle. Just go explain the situation.
Uh, excuse me, sir, but my friend was the one balling your wife a couple hours ago, and he seems to have left his wallet behind, so we were wondering...
Yeah! Like that. I’m giving you ten grand. Just go up there real polite and reason with him. You’re great at that.
So now the money has conditions?? Fuck you. And you said fifteen.
I’ll get it myself.
Hold on.
You don’t think I fuck you, bitch? I’ll fuck you.
I’m a bad girl. I’m a bad girl.
You picked him up and you fucked him, didn’t you, bitch?
I picked him up and I fucked him. I’m a bad girl.
And you liked fucking him, didn’t you, you fat little whore?
I liked it when you caught me fucking him.
The fuck was that?
The wallet! He took Derek’s wallet!
Get him!
Open up! Jesus! Open the goddamn door!
You motherfuckers! I’ll kill you! I’ll kill you motherfuckers!
You did it. You fucking did it.
Housekeeping.
Hey, Jack? Jack?
Hmmm?
That was quite a day yesterday.
Yep. Quite a day. Quite a week.
So are you going to follow up with Maya? Why don’t you invite her to the wedding?
Somehow I don’t think inviting Maya to your wedding is the right move, do you? In fact, I have the distinct suspicion she’s never going to want to see me again.
She’ll get over it. Chicks get over stuff like that. She digs you.
Hey, let me drive.
I’m fine. Why don’t you rest?
I don’t know. I feel like driving.
What’s wrong?
Buckle up, okay?
What the fuck!
You’re the one who said it looked like a car accident.
You’re a menace!
I’ll pay for it.
Look what you did!
I don’t know. Doesn’t look like anybody got hurt in this one.
Oh, no. No, you don’t.
You need a new car anyway.
I said I’d pay for it. Come on, Miles. Just this last thing.
You broke some.
Whatever. Sorry.
No, not whatever. You fucking derelict.
You ready?
Get it over with.
Oh, fuck!
Well. That about does it.
Why don’t you come in?
Uh-uh. You’re on your own.
Okay. So I’ll see you at the rehearsal.
Yeah.
Love you, man.
Back at you.
Hey, don’t pull away till they see the car.
You got it. Hey, why wasn’t I injured?
You were wearing your belt.
Do you have the rings?
Do you have the rings?
Hey, Miles.
Hi, Vicki. You look beautiful.
Thanks. Um, this is Ken Cortland, my husband.
How are you?
Hi. How you doing? You’re a lucky guy.
Thanks. I’ll wait for you in the car. Nice to meet you, Miles.
Ken.
That was big of him.
Yeah, he’s good that way. Very considerate.
That’s great.
So how’re you doing?
Since the last time we spoke? I don’t know. Could be better. Could be worse.
So what’s happening with your book?
Universally rejected. I’m three for three.
That’s awful. Oh, Miles. What are you going to do?
Back to the drawing board, I guess. Or not. So...you’re married. Congratulations. You look happy.
I am.
Seems like everyone’s getting married A year ago it was all divorces. Now it’s all weddings. Cyclical, I guess.
I guess.
Well, let’s go have some champagne, shall we? Toast all the newlyweds.
Not me. I’m not drinking.
You quit drinking?
I’m pregnant.
Oh. Huh. Well... Congratulations again, Vicki. That’s wonderful news.
See you over there, Miles.
Yeah.
“The marrow of his bone,” I repeated aimlessly. This at least penetrated my mind. Phineas had died from the marrow of his bone flowing down his blood stream to his heart.

I did not cry then or ever about Finny. I did not cry even when I stood watching him being lowered into his family’s straight-laced burial ground outside of Boston. I could not escape the feeling that this was my own funeral, and you do not cry in that case.
Do you want me to keep reading the next chapter, Mr. Raymond?
Hmmm? No, we’ll pick it up there on Monday.
One new message.
Hello, Miles. It’s Maya.
Thanks for your letter. I would have called you sooner, but I think I’ve needed some time to think about everything that happened and what you said and the possibility of seeing you again.

I think it’s really great, Miles. You’re so good with words. Who cares if it’s not getting published? There are so many beautiful and painful things about it. Did you really go through all that? It must have been hard. And the sister character -- Jesus. But I have to say I was really confused by the ending. Did she commit suicide, or did he kill her? It’s driving me crazy. And the title. Anyway, it’s turned cold and rainy here lately. But I like winter, and I’m thinking about --
The ending? That’s the whole point.
